name: Chronic Kidney Disease - Lab-Only Patients (No Diagnosis)
description: Tricky test case: finds patients with reduced eGFR (< 60 mL/min/1.73 m²) but NO chronic kidney disease diagnosis on their problem list (Path C). This is the dominant real-world undercoding pattern in CKD: patients are flagged biochemically as having Stage 3+ kidney function but never receive a formal CKD diagnosis. Studies estimate 30-50% of CKD Stage 3 patients are undiagnosed in primary care. Discoverable only by Observation queries with value-quantity filters combined with set difference against Condition queries.
prompt: Are there any patients in our system who have reduced eGFR values (below 60 mL/min/1.73 m²) suggesting CKD stage 3 or worse, but have not been formally diagnosed with chronic kidney disease? I want to identify potential undiagnosed CKD for nephrology referral.

prompts.naive: Find patients with low eGFR but no CKD diagnosis.
prompts.expert: I need to identify the dominant undercoding pattern in CKD: patients with biochemical evidence of reduced kidney function who lack a formal CKD Condition resource. Two-step approach: first find all patients with Observation for eGFR (LOINC 62238-1) where value-quantity is less than 60, then exclude any patient with a Condition for CKD subtypes (709044004, 431856006, 433144002, 700378005, 431857002). The remaining patients are candidates for nephrology referral and CKD diagnosis coding.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path C 'tricky' patients: have reduced eGFR but no CKD Condition resource. The Synthea module weights this path heavily (~35%) because real-world CKD is substantially undercoded — primary care often does not formally diagnose CKD even when labs meet criteria.

required_codes.display: Glomerular filtration rate [Volume Rate/Area] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Creatinine-based formula (CKD-EPI)/1.73 sq M
